# daytona_runner (Resource)

Manages a Daytona runner registration.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "daytona_runner" "example" {
  region_id = daytona_region.example.id
  name      = "example-runner"

  tags = ["terraform"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Runner name.
- `region_id` (String) Region ID where the runner is registered.

### Optional

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `draining` (Boolean, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Sets Daytona draining mode for the runner. Daytona accepts this value through the API but does not return it in runner reads, so Terraform treats it as write-only.
- `tags` (List of String) Tags associated with the runner.
- `unschedulable` (Boolean) Whether Daytona should stop scheduling new work on the runner.

### Read-Only

- `api_key` (String, Sensitive) Runner API key returned when the runner is created.
- `cpu` (String) Runner CPU capacity.
- `created_at` (String) Runner creation timestamp.
- `disk` (String) Runner disk capacity in GiB.
- `gpu` (String) Runner GPU capacity, when available.
- `gpu_type` (String) Runner GPU type, when available.
- `id` (String) Daytona runner ID.
- `memory` (String) Runner memory capacity in GiB.
- `region` (String) Runner region name.
- `state` (String) Current runner state.
- `updated_at` (String) Runner update timestamp.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import daytona_runner.example "runner-id"
```
